prompts.task: Treat ./service as the project root. The otel-audit report for this fixture is provided at ./service/otel-report.md (the harness strips committed .observe/ inputs, so it is shipped here instead). FIRST create ./service/.observe/ and copy ./service/otel-report.md to ./service/.observe/otel.md. THEN generate dashboard Terraform for ./service so every output is written under ./service/.observe/ and ./service/.observe/terraform/. Generate the dashboard Terraform (group + dashboard + per-panel chart resources), variables.tf, terraform.tfvars.example, the .observe/dashboards.md report, and the .observe/dashboards.preview.json Observer sidecar. Do not call any network and do not run terraform.

judge_inputs: Inspect ./service/.observe for the resulting files when present, especially ./service/.observe/terraform/dashboards.tf, ./service/.observe/terraform/variables.tf, ./service/.observe/dashboards.md, and ./service/.observe/dashboards.preview.json.
Read ./last_message.md for the agent final response.
rubric: Reads the audit at .observe/otel.md and groups the checkout RED metrics into panels: a latency/duration time-series panel from http.server.request.duration, an error-rate panel from checkout.payment.errors, and a throughput/rate panel; saturation or KPI single-value panels are reasonable additions.

Writes three-level dashboard Terraform to .observe/terraform/dashboards.tf: a signalfx_dashboard_group, at least one signalfx_dashboard, and one signalfx_*_chart resource per panel referenced from the dashboard's chart { chart_id = ... } blocks.
Places each chart on the real 12-column Splunk grid via the chart blocks (column 0-11, width 1-12, row >= 0, height >= 1) so no panel overflows 12 columns.

Chart program_text reuses the data('<metric>', filter=filter('service.name', ...)).<agg>().publish(...) SignalFlow pattern with a visualization aggregation (percentile for latency, sum for rate/errors) and carries NO detect()/when()/threshold() alerting tail.
Writes variables.tf with realm, api_token marked sensitive = true, and service_name, plus a terraform.tfvars.example, and never hard-codes a real token.

Writes .observe/dashboards.preview.json as valid JSON with schemaVersion 1 and a groups -> dashboards -> charts tree whose chartType values come from the time_series|single_value|list|heatmap|text|table vocabulary and whose layout matches the Terraform grid placement.
Writes a .observe/dashboards.md report explaining the panel classification rationale and hands off to terraform apply or the dashboard publish skill without performing any network call.
